response:
THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED AS FOLLOWS:  
THE FIRST CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS OF $ r $ CM, AND EACH SUCCESSIVE CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS THAT IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS ONE.  
THE RADIUS OF THE SECOND CIRCLE IS $ 1.1r $, THE THIRD IS $ 1.21r $, AND SO ON, UNTIL THE EIGHTH CIRCLE, WHICH HAS A RADIUS OF $ 1.771561 r $.  
THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS $ \pi \times \text{radius}^2 $.  
THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF THE AREAS OF ALL 8 CIRCLES:  
$ \pi r^2 + \pi (1.1r)^2 + \pi (1.21r)^2 + \pi (1.331r)^2 + \pi (1.4641r)^2 + \pi (1.61051r)^2 + \pi (1.771561r)^2 + \pi (1.9487171r)^2 $.  
THIS CAN BE SIMPLIFIED TO:  
$ \pi r^2 \times (1 + 1.21 + 1.4641 + 1.771561 + 2.14358881 + 2.46011151 + 2.82429536 + 3.43082746) $.  
THE SUM OF THE RADII SQUARES IS:  
$ 1 + 1.21 + 1.4641 + 1.771561 + 2.14358881 + 2.46011151 + 2.82429536 + 3.43082746 = 14.71561413 $.  
THE TOTAL AREA IS:  
$ \pi r^2 \times 14.71561413 $.  
THIS IS THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES.
